subject: Chemistry
chapter: Organic Chemistry: Some Basic Principles and Techniques

remember: Students will be able to recall the definition, types, and applications of isomers.
Students will be able to identify and explain the basic principles of functional groups.
Students will be able to list the main characteristics of aliphatic and aromatic hydrocarbons.
understand: Students will be able to explain the concept of hybridization and bonding in organic compounds.
Students will be able to understand the difference between saturated and unsaturated hydrocarbons.
Students will be able to describe the preparation, properties, and uses of alcohols and ethers.
apply: Students will be able to apply the IUPAC nomenclature rules to name organic compounds.
Students will be able to use the concept of functional groups to predict the properties of organic compounds.
Students will be able to deduce the structure of an organic compound from its molecular formula and functional group.
analyze: Students will be able to analyze the structure-activity relationship of organic compounds.
Students will be able to evaluate the importance of stereoisomerism in organic chemistry.
Students will be able to compare and contrast the properties of different types of organic compounds.

ncert_objectives: Explain the basic principles of functional groups and their classification.
Describe the preparation, properties, and uses of alcohols and ethers.
Explain the concept of hybridization and bonding in organic compounds.
board_exam_objectives: Define and explain the types of isomerism (structural and stereoisomerism).
Describe the preparation, properties, and uses of aliphatic and aromatic hydrocarbons.
Explain the concept of functional groups and their classification.
